section_b_your_plan.summary: A kids birthday party for about 20 guests (12 kids and 8 adults) at a backyard / home in Honolulu. It's paced to be easy to run and safe for young children. The plan walks you through 24 preparation steps, 8 day-of steps, and 2 wrap-up steps, with a budget estimate, key safety reminders, and ready-to-send messages.
section_b_your_plan.headline: Superhero Kids Birthday Party

timeline.name: Foundations
timeline.goal: Date, guest list, budget, venue, needs

timeline.name: Bookings & Invites
timeline.goal: Invitations sent; cake, food, décor, flow decided

timeline.name: Preparation
timeline.goal: RSVPs confirmed; supplies bought; quantities finalized

timeline.name: Final days
timeline.goal: Fresh shopping; cake ready; partial setup

timeline.name: Day-of
timeline.goal: Decorate, run the party, wind down

timeline.name: Wrap-up
timeline.goal: Cleanup, thank-yous

preparation_checklist.task: Confirm date & time window
Decide guest count / list
Set budget target
Confirm venue / location
Capture dietary/allergy & accessibility needs
Plan supervision: set adult-to-child ratio and assign watchers
Send invitations with RSVP + allergy ask
Decide & order the cake
Plan the menu (food & drinks)
Plan decorations
Plan the run-of-day (arrival → activities → food → cake → wind-down)
Confirm parent presence (stay vs drop-off) and collect emergency contacts
Plan age-appropriate games (simple, short, plus 1–2 backups)
Keep the schedule short & well-paced (1.5–2h; alternate active/calm; build in wind-down)
Track & confirm RSVPs
Buy supplies, tableware & décor
Confirm dietary/allergy-safe provisioning
Finalize headcount-based quantities
Plan & buy favors / goodie bags (per child)
Shop for fresh food & drinks
Bake or collect the cake; store correctly
Pre-decorate / stage setup
Child-proof & safety-check the space (hazards, choking risks, boundaries, water)
Prepare first-aid kit + allergy action items; note nearest help

day_of_checklist.task: Decorate & set up stations
Set out food; allergy-safe items separated & labeled
Welcome guests; run the party
Cake & candles moment
Brief helpers on roles, ratio, and the allergy plan before guests arrive
Actively supervise during the party (headcounts, boundaries, water)
Calm wind-down before pickup (quiet activity; avoid over-tired meltdown)
Hand out favors at exit; reunite each child with parent/approved contact

meta.disclaimer: Illustrative placeholder seed values for Honolulu — not researched market rates. These estimate the EVENT's costs, never ActivLife Hub fees. Calibrate before launch.

section_c_budget.levers_note: Your biggest costs are party food and decorations. Keeping things simple stays near the low end ($300 USD); adding extras pushes toward the high end ($980 USD). A typical plan lands around $530 USD.

risks.name: Food allergy / dietary
risks.mitigation: Capture at RSVP (BC-T05/BC-T06); confirm allergy-safe provisioning (BC-T13); separate & label at service (BC-T19); keep an action plan.

risks.name: Child supervision lapse
risks.mitigation: Maintain ratio (ST1-T01); brief helpers (ST1-T08); active supervision + headcounts (ST1-T09); secure boundaries/water (ST1-T06).

risks.name: Choking / age-inappropriate hazard
risks.mitigation: Child-proof space + age-appropriate food/toys (ST1-T06); first-aid ready (ST1-T07).

risks.name: Headcount uncertainty / late RSVPs
risks.mitigation: Chase RSVPs (BC-T11); confirm count before fresh shopping (BC-T14/BC-T15).

risks.name: Budget overrun
risks.mitigation: Track spend vs target (BC-T03); cake and food are the main levers.

risks.name: Cake problem (late / melt / wrong)
risks.mitigation: Confirm pickup/bake timing (BC-T16); cool storage; have a backup dessert.

risks.name: Over-tired / over-sugared meltdown
risks.mitigation: Short paced schedule (ST1-T04); calm wind-down (ST1-T10); pace sweets/cake.

risks.name: Games fall flat (short attention span)
risks.mitigation: Simple short games with 1–2 backups (ST1-T03); pacing (ST1-T04).

invitation.text: You're invited to [honoree_name]'s event! Join us [date] at [time], at [location]. Please RSVP by [rsvp_deadline]. [allergy_ask]

reminder.text: Hi [guest_name], just a reminder about [honoree_name]'s event [date] at [time], at [location]. Hope to see you there!

thank_you.text: Thank you, [guest_name], for celebrating with [honoree_name]! It meant a lot to have you there.

feedback.text: Hi [guest_name]! We'd love your quick feedback — was the timing and setup good? Anything we could do better next time?

section_f_upgrade_path.text: You can run this yourself. If your guest list grows significantly (currently about 20 guests), or you'd rather have it fully handled, you can hand the plan to a certified organizer who can quote on it — you keep the plan either way.

coverage.reason: Supported activity within self-serve planning limits.

timeline.name: Foundations
timeline.goal: Date, guest list, budget, location, dietary needs

timeline.name: Planning & Invites
timeline.goal: Invites sent; menu, drinks, grill & fuel planned

timeline.name: Preparation
timeline.goal: RSVPs confirmed; supplies bought; quantities finalized

timeline.name: Final days
timeline.goal: Fresh shop + ice; prep; grill & food-safety ready

timeline.name: Day-of
timeline.goal: Set up, grill safely, serve, wind down

timeline.name: Wrap-up
timeline.goal: Cleanup, leftovers, thank-yous

preparation_checklist.task: Confirm date & time (consider season/weather)
Decide guest count / list
Set budget target
Confirm location
Capture dietary/allergy & non-meat needs
Send invitations with RSVP + dietary ask
Plan the menu (grill mains, sides, dessert)
Plan drinks, ice & coolers
Plan grill & fuel (type, capacity for headcount)
Plan the run-of-day (arrival → grill in batches → eat → wind-down)
Track & confirm RSVPs
Buy supplies (fuel, tools, coolers, foil, disposables, trash bags)
Confirm dietary/allergy-safe plan (separate veg/allergy items & utensils)
Finalize quantities (meat/veg per head, drinks, ice)
Shop for fresh food & drinks; buy ice
Prep marinades & sides; pre-cook where possible
Ready & check the grill; set up food-safety (coolers, thermometer, separate boards/tongs)

day_of_checklist.task: Set up grill station, food/drink tables, seating
Keep raw meat cold & separate; cook to safe temperatures; avoid cross-contamination
Grill in batches; serve (veg/allergy items separated & labeled); keep drinks flowing
Wind down; handle leftovers safely (don't leave perishables out too long)

after_event_checklist.task: Pack up & clean (grill cleanup, waste removal)

meta.disclaimer: Illustrative placeholder seed values for Honolulu — not researched market rates. These estimate the EVENT's costs, never ActivLife Hub fees. Calibrate before launch.

section_c_budget.levers_note: Your biggest costs are food and drinks. Keeping things simple stays near the low end ($315 USD); adding extras pushes toward the high end ($975 USD). A typical plan lands around $525 USD.

risks.name: Food safety (raw meat / cross-contamination / undercooking)
risks.mitigation: Keep raw meat cold (coolers); separate boards/tongs; cook to safe temperatures (thermometer); don't leave perishables out (BQ-T17/BQ-T19/BQ-T21).

risks.name: Food allergy / dietary
risks.mitigation: Capture at RSVP (BQ-T05/BQ-T06); confirm safe plan (BQ-T13); separate & label veg/allergy items and utensils at service (BQ-T20).

risks.name: Headcount uncertainty / no-shows
risks.mitigation: Chase RSVPs (BQ-T11); confirm before fresh shopping (BQ-T14/BQ-T15).

risks.name: Budget overrun
risks.mitigation: Track spend vs target (BQ-T03); food & drinks are the main levers.

risks.name: Running out of food / fuel / ice
risks.mitigation: Size quantities to headcount + buffer; carry spare fuel and ice (BQ-T12/BQ-T14).

section_b_your_plan.summary: A networking event for about 40 guests (0 kids and 40 adults) at your venue in Lisbon. The plan walks you through 18 preparation steps, 5 day-of steps, and 2 wrap-up steps, with a budget estimate, key safety reminders, and ready-to-send messages.
section_b_your_plan.headline: Networking Event

timeline.name: Foundations
timeline.goal: Purpose, audience, size, budget, format, date, venue, needs

timeline.name: Promotion & Invites
timeline.goal: Registration open; invites sent; run-of-show, icebreaker & refreshments planned

timeline.name: Preparation
timeline.goal: Registrations confirmed; name tags & hosts ready; quantities finalized

timeline.name: Final days
timeline.goal: Final numbers; materials printed; hosts briefed

timeline.name: Day-of
timeline.goal: Set up, welcome, run networking, close

timeline.name: Follow-up
timeline.goal: Thank-you, recap & contacts, feedback

preparation_checklist.task: Define purpose & target audience (who should connect, and why)
Decide expected size / guest list (10–50)
Set budget target
Choose format (structured, free, or a blend) and event length
Set date & time (e.g. an after-work window)
Confirm a venue with good flow for mingling
Capture accessibility / dietary / language needs
Open registration capturing name, organization & interests
Promote & invite the target audience
Plan the run-of-show (welcome → icebreaker → networking → close)
Plan an icebreaker / structured introductions to ease the first minutes
Plan light refreshments & drinks
Track & confirm registrations
Prepare name tags (name + organization / interest)
Recruit & line up hosts/greeters/facilitators (welcome solo arrivals, fold in newcomers, prevent cliques)
Finalize headcount-based quantities (name tags, refreshments, space)
Confirm final numbers; print name tags & signage
Brief hosts on roles, the run-of-show, and the inclusion plan before doors

day_of_checklist.task: Set up the space for mingling + check-in / name-tag table + greeter
Welcome & open: set tone, purpose, format and simple ground rules
Run the icebreaker / facilitated introductions
Actively host: introduce people, fold in solo arrivals/newcomers, prevent cliques, manage structured↔free transitions
Close properly: clear wrap-up, thank-you, and a follow-up call-to-action / next step

after_event_checklist.task: Send follow-up: thank-you + recap/resources (and shared contacts if opted in)

section_c_budget.fallback_note: A budget estimate is not available for this activity type yet — the rest of your plan is complete.

risks.name: Attendees arrive alone and are left stranded
risks.mitigation: Greeter at check-in (NW-T19); hosts actively welcome and fold in solo arrivals (NW-T15/NW-T18/NW-T22); opening icebreaker (NW-T21).

risks.name: Cliques form; newcomers excluded
risks.mitigation: Brief hosts to mix groups and include newcomers (NW-T15/NW-T18/NW-T22); structured introductions (NW-T11).

risks.name: No follow-up — connections fizzle and value is lost
risks.mitigation: Capture contacts & interests at registration (NW-T08); close with a clear next step (NW-T23); send the follow-up recap (NW-T24).

risks.name: Awkward, slow start / no early momentum
risks.mitigation: Warm welcome + simple ground rules (NW-T20); planned icebreaker / structured intros (NW-T11/NW-T21); greeter from the door (NW-T19).

risks.name: Low or uncertain turnout / no-shows
risks.mitigation: Target the right audience and over-invite (NW-T09); reminders and confirmation (NW-T13).

risks.name: Budget overrun
risks.mitigation: Track spend vs target (NW-T03); venue and refreshments are the main levers.

risks.name: Poor venue flow / layout kills mingling
risks.mitigation: Choose a venue with good flow (NW-T06); set up the space for mingling, not seating (NW-T19).

section_b_your_plan.summary: A networking event for about 40 guests (0 kids and 40 adults) at your venue in Honolulu. The plan walks you through 18 preparation steps, 5 day-of steps, and 2 wrap-up steps, with a budget estimate, key safety reminders, and ready-to-send messages.
section_b_your_plan.headline: Networking Event

timeline.name: Foundations
timeline.goal: Purpose, audience, size, budget, format, date, venue, needs

timeline.name: Promotion & Invites
timeline.goal: Registration open; invites sent; run-of-show, icebreaker & refreshments planned

timeline.name: Preparation
timeline.goal: Registrations confirmed; name tags & hosts ready; quantities finalized

timeline.name: Final days
timeline.goal: Final numbers; materials printed; hosts briefed

timeline.name: Day-of
timeline.goal: Set up, welcome, run networking, close

timeline.name: Follow-up
timeline.goal: Thank-you, recap & contacts, feedback

preparation_checklist.task: Define purpose & target audience (who should connect, and why)
Decide expected size / guest list (10–50)
Set budget target
Choose format (structured, free, or a blend) and event length
Set date & time (e.g. an after-work window)
Confirm a venue with good flow for mingling
Capture accessibility / dietary / language needs
Open registration capturing name, organization & interests
Promote & invite the target audience
Plan the run-of-show (welcome → icebreaker → networking → close)
Plan an icebreaker / structured introductions to ease the first minutes
Plan light refreshments & drinks
Track & confirm registrations
Prepare name tags (name + organization / interest)
Recruit & line up hosts/greeters/facilitators (welcome solo arrivals, fold in newcomers, prevent cliques)
Finalize headcount-based quantities (name tags, refreshments, space)
Confirm final numbers; print name tags & signage
Brief hosts on roles, the run-of-show, and the inclusion plan before doors

day_of_checklist.task: Set up the space for mingling + check-in / name-tag table + greeter
Welcome & open: set tone, purpose, format and simple ground rules
Run the icebreaker / facilitated introductions
Actively host: introduce people, fold in solo arrivals/newcomers, prevent cliques, manage structured↔free transitions
Close properly: clear wrap-up, thank-you, and a follow-up call-to-action / next step

after_event_checklist.task: Send follow-up: thank-you + recap/resources (and shared contacts if opted in)

recurrence.per_session_reminder: Send a reminder about 2 days before each session.

section_b_your_plan.summary: A fitness / yoga class for about 12 guests (0 kids and 12 adults) at your venue in Honolulu. The plan walks you through 6 preparation steps, 2 day-of steps, and 1 wrap-up steps, with a budget estimate, key safety reminders, and ready-to-send messages.
section_b_your_plan.headline: Fitness / Yoga Class

timeline.name: Plan the class
timeline.goal: Lock the format, instructor, venue and capacity

timeline.name: Promote & enrol
timeline.goal: Open registration and confirm participants

timeline.name: Prepare
timeline.goal: Ready the space and any materials

timeline.name: Run the session
timeline.goal: Welcome, teach, and wrap up

timeline.name: Follow up
timeline.goal: Thank participants and plan the next session

preparation_checklist.task: Confirm your instructor (or arrange one if you need help)
Decide capacity and book or confirm the venue
Open registration and confirm participant numbers
Send participants what to expect and what to bring
Ask participants about injuries or health conditions before the session
Prepare materials and equipment for the session

day_of_checklist.task: Set up the space before participants arrive
Run the session: welcome, teach, and close

after_event_checklist.task: Thank participants and share the next session details

meta.disclaimer: Sample reference pricing for Honolulu; adjust to local rates.

section_c_budget.levers_note: Your biggest costs are instructor fee and materials. Keeping things simple stays near the low end ($180 USD); adding extras pushes toward the high end ($1010 USD). A typical plan lands around $420 USD.

risks.name: Physical injury risk
risks.mitigation: Warm up, scale movements to ability, keep water and a first-aid kit on hand, and ask about injuries or conditions beforehand.

risks.name: Instructor suitability
risks.mitigation: Confirm the instructor has the right experience for this level and group size.
